title: 3 Syracuse council candidates, including ex-president, could be tossed from ballot
summary: Three Syracuse Democratic primary candidates for at-large city council, including a former council president, face legal challenges to their petition signatures that could result in their removal from the ballot. This election integrity matter involves potential procedural violations in the nomination process.

political_signal.description: Election process integrity scrutiny through petition validity challenges affecting Democratic primary candidacy

processing_notes: Article contains limited substantive details; specific candidate names and challenge grounds not provided in excerpt
Reference to 'ex-president' indicates former council leadership involved but not named
Four-way primary suggests competitive at-large race with potential outcome-altering legal determinations
City Clerk and Law Department likely involved in petition review and ballot access determinations
May warrant CIO tracking if election administration systems or transparency documentation processes are implicated
